response: Originating in the 1960s as a US Department of Defense project, the internet evolved from the 1969 ARPANET demonstration and the 1983 adoption of TCP/IP protocols into a foundational global network. The 1989 invention of the World Wide Web and the 1993 launch of the Mosaic browser democratized access, fueling the dot-com boom, social media expansion, and the mobile and cloud computing revolutions. Today, this continuous technological evolution connects over 5 billion people worldwide, serving as the indispensable infrastructure for modern commerce, communication, and daily life.
